title: Manager.prepare_model should set `manager` attribute
body:
In `Manager.prepare_model`, the `manager` attribute does not get set leading to an attribute error when attempting to call methods on a PodmanResource that was manually instantiated then prepared with `Manager.prepare_model`.

The expected behavior is that calling methods on resources prepared this way would not fail.

Here is a basic example:
```python
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.13"
# dependencies = [
#     "podman",
#     "pytest",
# ]
# ///
import podman
import podman.domain.pods
import pytest

# Change this to make the test work
POD_ID = "6f28816f05a1"


def test_normal_way():
    client = podman.PodmanClient()

    # Get the pod
    pod = client.pods.get(POD_ID)

    # This is successful
    pod.reload()


def test_observe_attribute_error():
    client = podman.PodmanClient()

    # Get the pod
    pod = podman.domain.pods.Pod({"Id": POD_ID})
    client.pods.prepare_model(pod)

    # raises AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'
    pod.reload()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__])
```

The implementation of `prepare_model` sets the `collection` attribute but does not set `manager`. The `collection` attribute appears to be an alias to `manager` in `PodmanResource.__init__`
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/435e7a904b92560b8c0d883ed8994f57eea27171/podman/domain/manager.py#L129

comments.body: Thanks for the investigation. I opened a PR, would you like to give me some feedback?

> Thanks for the investigation. I opened a PR, would you like to give me some feedback?

Wow that was fast! Thank you. I left [a comment](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/619#pullrequestreview-3684444526) on the PR.
